---
title: Linear Transformations
description: Matrices as functions that move vectors.
---

::: {.learning-objectives}
### Learning Objectives

- Interpret a matrix as a vector transformation.
- Recognize scaling, rotation, and projection examples.
- Connect transformations to geometry.
:::

## Concept

A linear transformation preserves vector addition and scalar multiplication. A
matrix is a compact way to describe such a transformation.

## Practice

::: {.practice-box}
1. What does `[[2, 0], [0, 2]]` do to every vector?
2. What does `[[1, 0], [0, 0]]` do in the plane?
3. Why are transformations useful for data and geometry?
:::
